notes: The game works well even in 4k. Only if I activate Shadow details and/or HDR and/or Bloom, the game will crash. If I set Environment details to high or middle, the rendering is crippled, low works. So this game is a little tricky!
title: Far Cry® 2: Fortune's Edition

notes: Runs perfectly, but I have to disable AA or it crashes instantly. Other reports say AA causes blocky artifacting anyway. Performance isn't great with high gfx settings but consistent 60fps achievable with slightly lower settings. Check PCGamingWiki for a couple mouse sensitivity tweaks if you have issues with that.
title: Far Cry® 2: Fortune's Edition

notes: I get frozen in a room right after I get saved by someone, can't do anything.
title: Far Cry® 2: Fortune's Edition

notes: All ok in menus. Crashes to start a game in loading screen. Return to desktop.
title: Far Cry® 2: Fortune's Edition

notes:
Far Cry 2 is a game with many issues - even on Windows. You'll be stuck with running this game in d3d9 which means pretty dreadful performance. You'll have to drop some settings down to medium to get the framerate high enough to be playable. On the topic of framerate, this game has countless issues when going over 60 FPS. Add "-RenderProfile_MaxFps 60" to your launch options to force a 60 FPS lock. 
Ingame anti-aliasing results in broken alpha textures. Not usable. 
D3D10 does not appear to work in any way. Not usable.
The game also crashes quite frequently. You'll almost never be able to use the buses due to crashing, and sometimes you'll crash out in the game world. Use the quick save, and use it often.
Once you've done all this, the game should be playable enough. You can follow the Steam guide to get the Predecessor Tapes in the game if you want more content, too.
title: Far Cry® 2: Fortune's Edition

notes: Game runs for a bit ( Showing "Far Cry 2 - Running" in Steam Library) then closes / stops.
title: Far Cry® 2: Fortune's Edition

notes: Worked very nicely. Ultra high preset still gave pretty good performance.
title: Far Cry® 2: Fortune's Edition

notes: Had to disable AA since causes blocky artifacting and enable the env variable PROTON_FORCE_LARGE_ADDRESS_AWARE on launch options, otherwise the game crashes after a while
title: Far Cry® 2: Fortune's Edition

notes: Game wasnt playable on newer versions of proton, ran perfectly on this version however I had to play on medium at 1080 with my RTX 2070 - as expected.
title: Far Cry® 2: Fortune's Edition

notes:
Disable esync, enable large address aware. Set everything to max, unfortunately the framerates are bad at times, no matter what the settings.

Antialiasing breaks texture rendering. So leave it off. 

Benchmark, when set to fullscreen, opens, then minimizes. Upon maximizing the window there are visible artefacts for up to several seconds before the game runs normally.
title: Far Cry® 2: Fortune's Edition

notes: As the WineD3D renderer has a lot of issues, I installed manually D9VK and it worked much better even with high/ultra settings, only anti-alias renders trees incorrectly.
title: Far Cry® 2: Fortune's Edition

notes:
This is nearly a gold rating. The only thing stopping it being, is some 'fixes' you have to do to stop it playing perfectly. I went through the list of other reports and tried them all. I will list them all here. Here are the launch options (not sure if they helped):
PROTON_NO_ESYNC
PROTON_NO_D3D10
PROTON_FORCE_LARGE_ADDRESS_AWARE
RenderProfile_MaxFps 60
I didn't see them fix anything for me in the game, I used them anyway. The best success I had was to, disable shadows, disable HDR and disable bloom effects. After that the game ran fine for me. It looks awful to me without shadows and another reason for the silver rating. Without disabling these three graphical options, the game just constantly crashed at random and everything you made a change in any menu. Lastly and importantly, disable anti ailiasing, the game crashes even more with it enabled. After that, it ran without issue
title: Far Cry® 2: Fortune's Edition

notes: I had to set PROTON_FORCE_LARGE_ADDRESS_AWARE=1 in the launch options to be able to run the game without crashes and then disabled anti-aliasing fot the trees to be rendered correctly. After that the game runs great in 4K with my GTX 970 from start to end. Even the level editor and the benckmark tool work!
title: Far Cry® 2: Fortune's Edition

notes: What works: Singleplayer (with problems), Hosting and joining multiplayer (LAN), Map editor. General performance: 80-120 FPS. Problems with Singleplayer: After collapsing in or leaving Pala right at the beginning of the story, you wake up in a safe house. But the game effectively stops working there: you can't move, you don't have any interface (although the Esc menu is working). Animations aren't frozen. That's game breaking, but here's a semi workaround: Get a savegame at 0% that was created with a working setup and paste it at <YOUR HOMEDIRECTORY>/.steam/steam/steamapps/compatdata/19900/pfx/drive_c/users/steamuser/My Documents/My Games/Far Cry 2/Saved Games . Did not test online multiplayer. For flawless stability I used this startup option PROTON_FORCE_LARGE_ADDRESS_AWARE=1 %command%
title: Far Cry® 2: Fortune's Edition

notes: Just copy paste PROTON_FORCE_LARGE_ADDRESS_AWARE=1 in the launch option. And voila! It's running flawlessly on low setting.
title: Far Cry® 2: Fortune's Edition
